seoTitle: PrideSilicon Products | Data Converters, Power Management & Motor Drivers | LiTong
seoDescription: PrideSilicon product catalog featuring precision data converters, power management ICs, motor drivers, and sensor interfaces for industrial and automotive applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does PrideSilicon offer?
faqs.answer: PrideSilicon offers four main product categories: Data Converters including precision SAR ADCs, sigma-delta ADCs, and high-resolution DACs for measurement and control applications; Power Management ICs including synchronous buck converters, LDO regulators, and power supervisors; Motor Drivers including integrated solutions for stepper motors, DC motors, and brushless DC motors; and Sensor Interfaces including precision operational amplifiers and instrumentation amplifiers for signal conditioning. All products are available in industrial and automotive temperature grades.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to explore PrideSilicon's complete portfolio, or contact our FAE team for product recommendations based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right PrideSilicon product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right PrideSilicon product depends on your specific application requirements. For data acquisition systems, consider our SAR ADCs for high-speed applications or sigma-delta ADCs for high-resolution measurement. For power management, evaluate input voltage range, output current requirements, and efficiency targets. For motor control applications, determine motor type (stepper, DC, or BLDC), voltage and current ratings, and control interface requirements. Our FAE team can provide detailed selection guidance based on your system specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application requirements for personalized product recommendations and reference designs.

faqs.question: What is the difference between industrial-grade and automotive-grade PrideSilicon products?
faqs.answer: Industrial-grade PrideSilicon products are qualified for -40°C to +85°C operation and meet industrial reliability standards. Automotive-grade products are AEC-Q100 qualified with enhanced reliability testing, wider temperature ranges (-40°C to +125°C or -40°C to +150°C), and comprehensive documentation for automotive applications. Both grades offer excellent performance, but automotive-grade is required for vehicle applications and includes additional qualification testing for temperature cycling, electromagnetic compatibility, and long-term reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select automotive-grade products for vehicle applications requiring AEC-Q100 qualification. Industrial-grade is suitable for general industrial and commercial applications.

faqs.question: Does PrideSilicon offer pin-compatible alternatives to other suppliers?
faqs.answer: Yes, many PrideSilicon products are designed to be pin-compatible with popular industry-standard devices, enabling easy migration without PCB redesign. This includes compatibility with major analog semiconductor suppliers for data converters, power management ICs, and motor drivers. Pin compatibility reduces design risk and accelerates time-to-market when transitioning to PrideSilicon solutions. Our products offer competitive performance with shorter lead times and better pricing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your current part numbers to identify pin-compatible PrideSilicon alternatives and receive migration support.

faqs.question: What package options are available for PrideSilicon products?
faqs.answer: PrideSilicon products are available in various industry-standard packages including SOT-23, MSOP, TSSOP, QFN, and SOIC. Package selection depends on thermal requirements, PCB space constraints, and manufacturing processes. QFN packages offer excellent thermal performance and small footprint for space-constrained designs. TSSOP and SOIC packages provide easy handling and inspection for prototyping. All packages meet industrial reliability requirements and are qualified for the specified temperature ranges.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider thermal requirements, PCB space, and manufacturing capabilities when selecting package types. Contact our FAE team for package selection guidance.

categories.name: Data Converters
categories.description: High-performance data converters for precision measurement and control applications
categories.longDescription: PrideSilicon's data converter portfolio delivers exceptional performance for demanding industrial and automotive applications. Our precision ADCs and DACs feature excellent linearity, low noise, and wide dynamic range to meet the requirements of modern measurement and control systems. The product line includes high-speed SAR ADCs for data acquisition systems, high-resolution sigma-delta ADCs for precision instrumentation, and multi-channel DACs for industrial control applications. All converters support standard digital interfaces including SPI and I2C for easy integration with microcontrollers and processors. Key features include resolution from 12-bit to 24-bit, sampling rates up to 1 MSPS, low power consumption for battery-powered applications, integrated voltage references and buffers, and industrial and automotive temperature grades. As an authorized PrideSilicon distributor, we provide comprehensive technical support including reference designs, application notes, and schematic review services to help you achieve optimal system performance.

series.name: PSA Series
series.description: Precision SAR ADCs for high-speed data acquisition

selectionGuide.title: Data Converter Selection Guide
selectionGuide.content: How to select the right data converter for your application
recommendations: Use SAR ADCs for high-speed applications
Use sigma-delta ADCs for precision measurement
Consider integrated PGAs for sensor applications

selectionGuideLink.text: How to select the right PrideSilicon data converter?
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting PrideSilicon data converters based on resolution, speed, and application requirements

faqs.question: What types of data converters does PrideSilicon offer?
faqs.answer: PrideSilicon offers three main types of data converters: SAR ADCs for high-speed applications requiring fast conversion times up to 1 MSPS; Sigma-Delta ADCs for high-resolution measurement applications demanding excellent noise performance up to 24-bit resolution; and DACs for precision control and signal generation. SAR ADCs are ideal for multiplexed systems and control loops. Sigma-delta converters excel in precision instrumentation with outstanding dynamic range and integrated programmable gain amplifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SAR ADCs for high-speed data acquisition and sigma-delta ADCs for precision measurement applications. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right resolution for my application?
faqs.answer: Resolution selection depends on your measurement accuracy requirements. For general industrial control, 12-bit resolution provides sufficient accuracy. For precision instrumentation, 16-bit resolution offers 0.0015% accuracy suitable for most test equipment. For high-precision measurement like weigh scales and medical devices, 20-24 bit resolution provides the necessary accuracy. Consider the full signal chain including sensor accuracy, signal conditioning, and ADC resolution to achieve optimal system performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate your required accuracy based on system specifications, then select ADC resolution with margin. Our FAE team can help with resolution calculations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SAR and sigma-delta ADCs?
faqs.answer: SAR ADCs use successive approximation to achieve fast conversion times, making them ideal for multiplexed systems and applications requiring high sampling rates. They offer good resolution (12-16 bit) with low latency. Sigma-delta ADCs use oversampling and noise shaping to achieve very high resolution (16-24 bit) with excellent noise performance, but with lower sampling rates and higher latency. SAR ADCs are best for control systems and data acquisition, while sigma-delta ADCs excel in precision measurement and sensor applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select SAR ADCs for high-speed applications and sigma-delta ADCs for high-precision measurement. Contact our FAE team for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for PrideSilicon data converters?
faqs.answer: PrideSilicon data converters support multiple interface options to accommodate different system requirements. SPI is the most common interface, offering high-speed communication with simple implementation and full-duplex operation. I2C provides lower speed but requires fewer pins, making it suitable for space-constrained designs and systems with multiple devices. Parallel interfaces offer the highest throughput for demanding applications. All interfaces support standard protocol features and are compatible with popular microcontrollers and processors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SPI for high-speed applications, I2C for multi-device systems with pin constraints. Contact our FAE team for interface selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the effective number of bits (ENOB) for my application?
faqs.answer: ENOB represents the actual resolution achieved considering noise and distortion. It is calculated from the Signal-to-Noise and Distortion Ratio (SINAD) using the formula: ENOB = (SINAD - 1.76) / 6.02. For example, an ADC with 80 dB SINAD has approximately 13 ENOB. Factors affecting ENOB include input signal frequency, reference noise, power supply quality, and PCB layout. PrideSilicon ADCs are characterized for ENOB across temperature and frequency to ensure consistent performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate ENOB at your operating conditions to ensure the ADC meets accuracy requirements. Contact our FAE team for ENOB analysis and layout recommendations.

products.name: 16-Bit 1MSPS SAR ADC
products.shortDescription: High-speed 16-bit SAR ADC with 1 MSPS sampling rate, SPI interface, and low power consumption for industrial data acq...
descriptionParagraphs: The PSA1604 is a high-performance 16-bit successive approximation register (SAR) analog-to-digital converter designed for demanding industrial and automotive applications.
With a maximum sampling rate of 1 MSPS and excellent DC accuracy, this ADC delivers reliable performance for data acquisition systems, process control, and test equipment.
The device features a SPI-compatible serial interface, integrated voltage reference, and low power consumption making it ideal for portable and battery-powered applications.
features: 1 MSPS sampling rate with low latency
Low power consumption: 15 mW at 1 MSPS

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Precision Analog
faeReview.content: In my 12 years supporting precision analog designs, the PSA1604 stands out as an excellent choice for industrial data acquisition. I particularly appreciate its combination of high speed and accuracy - the 1 MSPS sampling rate with 90 dB SNR is impressive for a 16-bit SAR ADC. The integrated voltage reference simplifies design and reduces BOM cost. I've successfully used this device in motor control applications where fast, accurate current sensing is critical. The SPI interface is straightforward to implement, and the low power consumption makes it suitable for portable equipment. For best performance, I recommend using a solid ground plane and placing decoupling capacitors close to the device.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent speed-accuracy balance with integrated reference

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative for applications not requiring high speed
alternativeParts.useCase: Suitable for low-speed data logging and monitoring applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher resolution alternative for precision applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Best for high-precision measurement where speed is less critical

companionParts.description: 3.3V buck converter for ADC power supply
Precision op-amp for input signal conditioning
MCU with SPI interface for data processing

faqs.question: What is the maximum sampling rate of the PSA1604?
faqs.answer: The PSA1604 supports a maximum sampling rate of 1 MSPS (mega-samples per second) at 16-bit resolution. This high sampling rate enables capture of fast-changing signals in industrial control and data acquisition applications. The conversion time is approximately 1 microsecond, with minimal latency between conversions. At lower sampling rates, power consumption decreases proportionally, making it efficient for battery-powered applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 1 MSPS rate is ideal for motor control, vibration analysis, and fast data acquisition. For slower applications, consider lower-power alternatives.

faqs.question: What is the accuracy and linearity of the PSA1604?
faqs.answer: The PSA1604 delivers excellent accuracy with ±2 LSB integral nonlinearity (INL) and ±0.9 LSB differential nonlinearity (DNL), ensuring no missing codes across the entire input range. The device achieves 90 dB signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and -100 dB total harmonic distortion (THD), providing clean conversion for precision measurement applications. These specifications are maintained across the full operating temperature range of -40°C to +125°C for automotive-grade devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: This level of accuracy is suitable for precision instrumentation and industrial control. Contact our FAE team for calibration recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I interface the PSA1604 with my microcontroller?
faqs.answer: The PSA1604 uses a standard SPI-compatible 4-wire interface consisting of CS (chip select), SCLK (serial clock), SDI (serial data input), and SDO (serial data output). To initiate a conversion, pull CS low and provide 16 clock cycles on SCLK. The conversion result is output on SDO MSB-first. The interface supports clock rates up to 50 MHz, enabling fast data transfer. Many microcontrollers including STM32, PIC, and Arduino have built-in SPI peripherals that work seamlessly with this ADC.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hardware SPI if available for best performance. Our FAE team can provide reference code for popular microcontroller platforms.

faqs.question: What power supply options does the PSA1604 support?
faqs.answer: The PSA1604 operates from a single 2.7V to 5.25V power supply, providing flexibility for various system designs. The digital I/O levels are referenced to the supply voltage, making it compatible with both 3.3V and 5V logic systems. An integrated 4.096V voltage reference eliminates the need for an external reference in most applications. For best performance, use a clean, low-noise power supply with adequate decoupling capacitors (0.1uF ceramic and 10uF tantalum) placed close to the device pins.
faqs.decisionGuide: The wide supply range simplifies system design. Use 5V for best SNR performance, 3.3V for compatibility with modern digital systems.

faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for the PSA1604?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is essential for achieving specified performance. Key recommendations include: 1) Use a solid ground plane under the ADC and analog input circuitry; 2) Separate analog and digital ground planes, connecting at a single point near the ADC; 3) Place decoupling capacitors (0.1uF ceramic) within 2mm of power pins; 4) Route digital signals away from analog inputs; 5) Use differential routing for analog inputs to reject common-mode noise; 6) Keep the reference pin quiet with dedicated filtering. Following these guidelines ensures optimal noise performance and accuracy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Careful layout is critical for precision ADCs. Our FAE team can review your PCB layout and provide optimization recommendations.

products.name: 24-Bit Sigma-Delta ADC
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 24-bit sigma-delta ADC with integrated PGA, excellent noise performance for precision measurement
descriptionParagraphs: The PSS2416 is a precision 24-bit sigma-delta analog-to-digital converter designed for high-resolution measurement applications.
Featuring an integrated programmable gain amplifier (PGA) and outstanding noise performance, this ADC is ideal for sensor interfaces, weigh scales, and industrial process control.
The device offers multiple output data rates, flexible input multiplexing, and a simple I2C interface for easy integration into embedded systems.
features: Integrated PGA with gains from 1 to 128
Low offset and gain drift over temperature
I2C interface with up to 400 kHz speed

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Sensor Systems
faeReview.content: The PSS2416 is my go-to recommendation for precision measurement applications. The integrated PGA with gains up to 128 is a game-changer for sensor interfaces, allowing direct connection to low-level signals without external amplification. I've used this ADC in weigh scale designs where the 24-bit resolution and low noise (40 nVrms) enable accurate measurements down to microvolt levels. The offset drift of only 0.05 uV/°C ensures stable performance across temperature. For best results, I recommend using the internal reference for ratiometric measurements and implementing proper input filtering. The I2C interface makes it easy to integrate with modern microcontrollers.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated PGA with excellent noise performance for sensor interfaces

comparison.pgaGain: Limited gain options vs full 1-128 range

alternativeParts.reason: Alternative for weigh scale applications with SPI preference
alternativeParts.useCase: Suitable for weigh scales and load cell applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative for simple weigh scale applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Best for basic weigh scales with cost constraints

companionParts.description: Low-noise op-amp for sensor signal conditioning
WiFi MCU with I2C for IoT applications
Evaluation module with load cell interface

faqs.question: What is the effective resolution of the PSS2416 at different PGA gains?
faqs.answer: The PSS2416 maintains excellent effective resolution across all PGA gain settings. At gain=1, the effective resolution is approximately 20 bits with 40 nVrms noise. At higher gains, the noise remains constant in input-referred terms, so the effective resolution improves. At gain=128, the device can resolve microvolt-level signals with effective resolution exceeding 22 bits. The integrated PGA eliminates the need for external amplification, reducing component count and improving accuracy by minimizing offset errors from external amplifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use higher gains for low-level sensor signals. The PGA allows direct connection to load cells and strain gauges without external amplifiers.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the output data rate for my application?
faqs.answer: The PSS2416 offers programmable output data rates from 5 SPS to 4 kSPS. Lower data rates provide better noise performance due to increased oversampling ratio. For example, at 5 SPS the noise is approximately 40 nVrms, while at 1 kSPS it increases to about 120 nVrms. The data rate is configured through the I2C interface by setting the appropriate configuration register. Consider your application requirements: weigh scales typically use 10-80 SPS for stability, while temperature monitoring may use 5-20 SPS for lower noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select lower data rates for better noise performance in precision applications. Higher rates are suitable for faster changing signals.

faqs.question: What is the best way to connect a load cell to the PSS2416?
faqs.answer: For load cell applications, connect the bridge output directly to the differential inputs of the PSS2416. The integrated PGA provides the necessary gain (typically 64 or 128 for load cells). Use the internal voltage reference in ratiometric configuration by connecting the reference output to the load cell excitation. This configuration eliminates errors from reference voltage variations. Add RC filters (100Ω + 0.1uF) at the inputs to reduce EMI. Place the ADC close to the load cell to minimize noise pickup in the low-level signal lines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ratiometric measurement with internal reference for best accuracy. Our FAE team can provide complete load cell interface reference designs.

faqs.question: How do I minimize temperature drift in precision measurements?
faqs.answer: The PSS2416 features excellent temperature stability with offset drift of only 0.05 uV/°C and gain drift of 0.5 ppm/°C. To minimize overall system drift: 1) Use the internal reference which has low temperature coefficient; 2) Implement ratiometric measurement for sensor applications; 3) Maintain consistent PCB temperature across the ADC and sensor; 4) Use low-drift external components if needed; 5) Consider system-level calibration for highest accuracy. These techniques can achieve measurement stability better than 10 ppm/°C in typical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: The low intrinsic drift often eliminates need for external compensation. For highest precision, implement system calibration at known temperatures.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between the PSS2416 and competing 24-bit ADCs?
faqs.answer: The PSS2416 offers several advantages over competing 24-bit ADCs: 1) Full PGA gain range from 1-128 vs limited options in competitors; 2) Lower power consumption at 0.9 mW vs 2-3 mW typical; 3) Standard I2C interface vs proprietary interfaces; 4) Better offset drift at 0.05 uV/°C; 5) Multiple input channels with internal multiplexer; 6) Competitive pricing with shorter lead times. These features make it ideal for multi-channel sensor systems and battery-powered applications where power and cost are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose PSS2416 for multi-channel sensor systems requiring low power and cost-effective solution with standard interface.

products.name: 12-Bit 8-Channel SAR ADC
products.shortDescription: Multi-channel 12-bit SAR ADC with 500kSPS sampling rate, ideal for industrial monitoring systems
descriptionParagraphs: The PSA1208 is an 8-channel 12-bit successive approximation register ADC designed for multi-channel data acquisition applications.
With a sampling rate of 500kSPS and integrated 8:1 multiplexer, this ADC is ideal for industrial monitoring, building automation, and test systems.
The device features automatic channel sequencing, programmable gain amplifier, and low power consumption for battery-powered applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Precision Analog
faeReview.content: The PSA1208 is an excellent choice for multi-channel data acquisition applications. The integrated multiplexer and automatic sequencing simplify system design by eliminating the need for external switching. I've used this ADC in building automation systems where it monitors temperature, humidity, and pressure sensors across 8 channels. The 500kSPS rate provides good throughput for most monitoring applications. The integrated PGA is useful for sensors with different output ranges. For best performance, I recommend using a solid ground plane and keeping analog input traces short and symmetric.
faeReview.highlight: 8-channel multiplexer with automatic sequencing for multi-sensor systems

products.name: 16-Bit 4-Channel DAC
products.shortDescription: Quad 16-bit DAC with voltage output, SPI interface, and high drive capability for industrial control
descriptionParagraphs: The PSD1612 is a quad 16-bit digital-to-analog converter with voltage output and high drive capability.
Featuring 16-bit monotonic performance and 10μs settling time, this DAC is ideal for industrial control, waveform generation, and setpoint control.
The device includes an internal reference, power-on reset, and rail-to-rail output buffers capable of driving 10mA loads.

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Sensor Systems
faeReview.content: The PSD1612 is my go-to recommendation for multi-channel analog output applications. The 16-bit resolution provides excellent control precision, and the 10μs settling time is fast enough for most industrial control loops. I particularly like the high drive capability - the 10mA output can directly drive many loads without external buffers. The internal reference simplifies design and reduces component count. I've used this DAC in process control systems where it generates setpoints for multiple control loops. The SPI interface makes it easy to integrate with modern microcontrollers.
faeReview.highlight: Quad 16-bit DAC with high drive capability and fast settling

categories.name: Power Management
categories.description: High-efficiency power management ICs for industrial and automotive applications
categories.longDescription: PrideSilicon's power management portfolio delivers efficient and reliable power conversion solutions for demanding industrial and automotive applications. Our DC-DC converters and LDO regulators feature high efficiency, low noise, and excellent transient response to meet the requirements of modern electronic systems. The product line includes synchronous buck converters for high-efficiency step-down conversion, low-dropout regulators for clean analog power, and power supervisors for system monitoring. All power management ICs support wide input voltage ranges and offer protection features including overcurrent, overtemperature, and short-circuit protection. Key features include efficiency up to 95%, switching frequencies up to 2 MHz, low quiescent current for battery-powered applications, and industrial and automotive temperature grades. As an authorized PrideSilicon distributor, we provide comprehensive technical support including reference designs, application notes, and power supply design services to help you achieve optimal system performance.

selectionGuide.title: Power Management Selection Guide
selectionGuide.content: How to select the right power management IC for your application
recommendations: Use buck converters for high-efficiency step-down
Use LDOs for low-noise analog power
Consider switching frequency for EMI requirements

selectionGuideLink.text: How to select the right PrideSilicon power management IC?
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting PrideSilicon power management ICs based on efficiency, noise, and application requirements

faqs.question: What types of power management ICs does PrideSilicon offer?
faqs.answer: PrideSilicon offers two main types of power management ICs: Synchronous Buck Converters for high-efficiency step-down conversion with output currents up to 3A and efficiencies up to 95%; and Low-Dropout Regulators (LDOs) for clean, low-noise power supply with high PSRR and fast transient response. Buck converters are ideal for battery-powered applications and high-current digital loads. LDOs excel in analog and RF applications requiring clean power with minimal noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose buck converters for high-efficiency applications and LDOs for low-noise analog power. Contact our FAE team for power supply design guidance.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the efficiency of a buck converter?
faqs.answer: Buck converter efficiency is calculated as the ratio of output power to input power: Efficiency = (Vout × Iout) / (Vin × Iin) × 100%. PrideSilicon buck converters achieve up to 95% efficiency at optimal operating points. Efficiency varies with load current, input voltage, and component selection. Light load efficiency is enhanced by automatic power-save modes that reduce switching losses. For battery-powered applications, consider both active mode efficiency and quiescent current during standby.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select converters with efficiency curves matching your load profile. Our FAE team can help optimize component selection for maximum efficiency.

faqs.question: What is the difference between synchronous and non-synchronous buck converters?
faqs.answer: Synchronous buck converters use a MOSFET instead of a diode for the low-side switch, reducing conduction losses and improving efficiency, especially at low output voltages. Non-synchronous converters use a diode, which is simpler but less efficient. PrideSilicon's synchronous buck converters offer higher efficiency (up to 95%), better thermal performance, and the ability to sink current for faster transient response. The integrated MOSFETs also reduce component count and board space.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select synchronous converters for high-efficiency and low-voltage applications. Contact our FAE team for converter selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right LDO for my application?
faqs.answer: LDO selection depends on several factors: 1) Input-output voltage differential (dropout voltage) - select LDOs with dropout voltage lower than your minimum headroom; 2) Output current - ensure the LDO can deliver required current with adequate margin; 3) Noise and PSRR - for sensitive analog circuits, select low-noise LDOs with high PSRR at relevant frequencies; 4) Quiescent current - for battery applications, select low-Iq LDOs; 5) Package - consider thermal requirements and board space.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate dropout voltage, noise requirements, and current needs. Our FAE team can recommend optimal LDOs for your specific application.

faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for switching regulators?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is critical for switching regulator performance. Key recommendations include: 1) Minimize the loop area formed by input capacitor, high-side switch, and low-side switch to reduce EMI; 2) Place input capacitors close to the IC pins; 3) Use wide, short traces for high-current paths; 4) Keep sensitive analog signals away from switching nodes; 5) Use a solid ground plane and multiple vias for thermal management; 6) Place output capacitors close to the load for best transient response.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow layout guidelines for minimal EMI and optimal performance. Our FAE team can review your PCB layout and provide recommendations.

products.name: 3A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency 3A synchronous buck converter with wide input range, adjustable output, and excellent transient response
descriptionParagraphs: The PSP3406 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter capable of delivering up to 3A continuous output current.
With a wide input voltage range of 4.5V to 36V and adjustable output from 0.8V to 24V, this converter is ideal for industrial and automotive power applications.
The device features peak current mode control, internal compensation, and protection features including overcurrent, overtemperature, and short-circuit protection.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The PSP3406 is an excellent choice for industrial power applications. I particularly like the wide input voltage range that handles both 12V and 24V industrial buses without modification. The efficiency reaches 95% at mid-loads, which significantly reduces thermal management requirements. I've used this converter in battery-powered applications where the 25 uA quiescent current helps extend battery life. The internal compensation simplifies design - just add input and output capacitors and you're good to go. For EMI-sensitive applications, I recommend using the 500 kHz switching frequency and adding a small LC filter on the output.
faeReview.highlight: Wide input range with high efficiency and simple design

alternativeParts.comparison: PSP3406=><TPS54331: Input voltage 28V max < 36V (PrideSilicon has wider range), Output current 3A = 3A (same), Frequency Fixed vs adjustable (PrideSilicon more flexible), Efficiency 92% < 95% (PrideSilicon is more efficient), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Alternative with similar performance but narrower input range
alternativeParts.useCase: Suitable for 12V industrial applications with cost constraints

alternativeParts.comparison: PSP3406=><MP1584: Input voltage 28V max < 36V (PrideSilicon has wider range), Output current 3A = 3A (same), Frequency Higher fixed frequency, Package Smaller package available, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.useCase: Best for portable applications requiring small solution size

companionParts.description: External sync option for multi-phase applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum output current of the PSP3406?
faqs.answer: The PSP3406 can deliver up to 3A continuous output current with adequate thermal management. The device features cycle-by-cycle current limiting at 4.5A typical to protect against overload conditions. For reliable operation, ensure the junction temperature remains below 125°C by providing adequate heatsinking or airflow. The maximum output current may need to be derated at high ambient temperatures or with high input-output voltage differentials.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 3A rating is suitable for most industrial loads. For higher currents, consider parallel operation or contact our FAE team for alternative solutions.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage of the PSP3406?
faqs.answer: The PSP3406 output voltage is set using a resistor divider from the output to the FB pin. The formula is: Vout = 0.8V × (1 + R1/R2), where 0.8V is the internal reference voltage. For example, for 3.3V output with R2 = 10kΩ, R1 = 31.25kΩ. Use 1% tolerance resistors for accurate output voltage. The feedback pin is high impedance, so place the divider close to the IC to minimize noise pickup.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard resistor values or our online calculator for precise voltage setting. Contact our FAE team for voltage margining applications.

faqs.question: What input and output capacitors are recommended for the PSP3406?
faqs.answer: For input capacitors, use at least 10uF ceramic with X5R or X7R dielectric, rated for the maximum input voltage. Place the capacitor close to the VIN and GND pins to minimize switching noise. For output capacitors, use 22uF to 100uF ceramic depending on load transient requirements. Larger capacitance improves transient response but may affect loop stability. Use capacitors with low ESR for best performance. Additional bulk capacitance may be needed for applications with long input leads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 22uF output capacitance and adjust based on transient requirements. Our FAE team can help optimize capacitor selection.

faqs.question: How do I optimize the PSP3406 for EMI performance?
faqs.answer: To optimize EMI performance: 1) Use the lowest switching frequency that meets your size requirements (500 kHz produces less EMI than 2 MHz); 2) Minimize the switching node area to reduce radiated emissions; 3) Use shielded inductors or toroidal inductors to contain magnetic fields; 4) Add an RC snubber across the switching node if needed; 5) Use proper input filtering with a pi-filter for conducted emissions; 6) Follow good PCB layout practices with proper grounding. These techniques can achieve CISPR 22 Class B compliance with proper implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow EMI best practices from the start of design. Our FAE team can provide EMI-optimized reference designs and layout reviews.

faqs.question: What protection features does the PSP3406 include?
faqs.answer: The PSP3406 includes comprehensive protection features: Overcurrent Protection (OCP) with cycle-by-cycle current limiting at 4.5A typical; Thermal Shutdown at 165°C junction temperature with 15°C hysteresis to prevent thermal runaway; Short-Circuit Protection that limits current during output short conditions; Undervoltage Lockout (UVLO) that disables the converter when input voltage is too low; and Soft-Start that limits inrush current during startup. These features ensure reliable operation under fault conditions and protect both the converter and the load.
faqs.decisionGuide: The built-in protections simplify system design. For additional protection needs, contact our FAE team for external protection circuit recommendations.

products.name: 500mA Low Dropout Regulator
products.shortDescription: High-PSRR 500mA LDO with low noise, fast transient response for sensitive analog circuits
descriptionParagraphs: The PSLDO50 is a high-performance low-dropout voltage regulator designed for noise-sensitive analog and RF applications.
With high PSRR of 80dB at 1kHz and low output noise of 30uVrms, this LDO provides clean power for precision analog circuits.
The device features fast transient response, low dropout voltage of 200mV at 500mA, and excellent line and load regulation.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Analog Power
faeReview.content: The PSLDO50 is my top recommendation for sensitive analog applications. The 80dB PSRR at 1kHz effectively rejects switching noise from upstream converters, making it ideal for powering precision ADCs and DACs. I've used this LDO in medical imaging applications where the 30uVrms noise level is critical for image quality. The fast transient response handles load steps from ADC sampling without significant voltage droop. For best noise performance, I recommend adding a 10nF bypass capacitor at the BYP pin and keeping the output capacitor close to the load. The low quiescent current is also great for battery-powered portable equipment.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent PSRR and low noise for sensitive analog applications

alternativeParts.comparison: PSLDO50=><LP5907: Output current 250mA < 500mA (PrideSilicon higher current), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Lower noise alternative for ultra-sensitive applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Best for applications requiring lowest possible noise
